BREVARD FIRE DEPT REPORTED A MOBILE HOME AT 574 SELICA RD. WAS INUNDATED BY 4-6 FEET OF FLOOD WATERS FROM SPANISH OAK BRANCH. NO ONE WAS HOME AT THE TIME. ACROSS SELICA RD. FLOODWATERS ENTERED A CRAWL SPACE. APPROXIMATELY 1/4 MILE UPSTREAM ALONG SPANISH OAK BRANCH NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BRYSON RD. AND TWINVIEW DR. A 24- INCH CULVERT WAS WASHED OUT STRANDING A RESIDENCE. FLOODWATERS AT THE CONFLUENCE OF SPANISH OAK BRANCH AND GLADY BRANCH NEAR 440 ISLAND FORD RD. INUNDATED TWO DRIVEWAYS AND ONE VEHICLE UP TO ITS HOOD AND STRANDED RESIDENTS.

A MCDOWELL COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT DAMAGE SURVEY, IN COORDINATION WITH THE NWS, DETERMINED THAT A SMALL EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG US 221 N IN ASHFORD, TRAVELING JUST OVER HALF A MILE. MOST OF THE DAMAGE CONSISTED OF LIMBS DOWN WITH SOME TREES UPROOTED, BUT SOME MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED, INCLUDING TO SHINGLES OF THE INN AT BLUE RIDGE AND SOME METAL SKIRTING AROUND THE BASE OF A MOBILE HOME. SOME SMALL OUTBUILDINGS ALSO SUSTAINED DAMAGE.
